# A blocked stage's prerequisite-failure message must (a) name what actually
# happened (failed / has not run yet / etc.), not the generic "has not succeeded"
# that read identically for every case, and (b) show a short commit, not the full
# SHA, matching breeze's short-SHA display convention everywhere else — this text
# arrives as a plain string in Response.Error, not a structured field the CLI can
# reformat itself.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

# build fails outright (/bin/false) — the RPC itself still succeeds, reporting
# StageFailed as data.
! exec breeze stage start release build 98229189fcee04765b9b6f19c1ca64c67c9e749d --as ci
stdout 'build: failed'
! stdout 'cancelled'

# test is blocked because build FAILED — the message must say so explicitly,
# with the commit shown short, not in full.
! exec breeze stage start release test 98229189fcee04765b9b6f19c1ca64c67c9e749d --as ci
stderr 'prerequisite "build" \(98229189fcee\) failed'
! stderr '98229189fcee04765b9b6f19c1ca64c67c9e749d'

# On a commit that's never been touched at all, the message must say so —
# "has not run yet", not "failed" (it never even ran) and not the generic
# "has not succeeded".
! exec breeze stage start release test deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef --as ci
stderr 'prerequisite "build" \(deadbeefdead\) has not run yet'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/false"]
  }
  stage "test" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/true"]
  }
}
